prompt: Set up a **TrainingOps Enrollment & Quality Console** that enables offline training providers to manage class registrations, assessments, contracts, and customer trust signals in a single browser-based workspace. The application supports four roles: Administrator (configures rules and templates), Staff Reviewer (processes registrations and disputes), Instructor (grades and reviews learner progress), and Learner (submits registrations, takes quizzes, and leaves reviews). The SPA presents a dashboard with Cards and Charts for key KPIs, a Table-driven Registration Queue with filters and batch actions, and Detail Drawers for audit history and reviewer comments. Registration runs on an explicit state machine: Draft → Submitted → Needs More Info → Under Review → Approved/Rejected, with optional Cancelled and Waitlisted; waitlist promotion triggers only when an approved seat becomes available and the class fill rate drops below 95%. Reviewers must leave a comment of at least 20 characters on rejections, and the system timestamps every transition in an immutable-looking audit timeline. The quiz center provides question bank CRUD with single choice, multiple choice, fill-in, and subjective questions; bulk import accepts local Excel/JSON files with schema validation (required columns: questionText, type, correctAnswer, difficulty 1–5, tags). Paper generation supports rule sets such as “30 questions, 40% difficulty 3, 40% difficulty 4, 20% difficulty 5, at least 1 per chapter,” then auto-grades objective items immediately while subjective items require instructor entry with a 0–10 rubric and optional notes; learners can favorite questions and maintain a wrong-question notebook linked to explanations. Content and review features include product/class reviews with 1–5 stars, text up to 2,000 characters, up to 6 images (JPG/PNG ≤ 2 MB each), one follow-up review within 14 days, plus Q&A, favorites, and browsing history; moderation uses an offline sensitive-word dictionary and risk flags, and abuse reports must be resolved with outcomes (dismissed/removed/warned) within 7 days. All functionality is implemented with **原生 HTML/CSS/JavaScript (纯前端，LocalStorage持久化)** as a responsive single-page application using hash-based routing, Modal confirmations, and client-side form validation; no server, network calls, or third-party services are allowed. Business logic is handled by a frontend “service layer” that reads local JSON configuration (workflow rules, sensitive-word lists, scoring weights) and computes reputation scores from fulfillment rate, late rate, and complaint rate over the last 90 days; for example, a reputation score below 60 blocks new order-taking/booking privileges and forces manual review for future registrations. Two-way ratings are captured after completion, include tag-based feedback, and support an appeal flow where a Staff Reviewer can uphold/adjust/void a rating with a written rationale logged to the audit trail. Contract/document templates support variable placeholders (e.g., {LearnerName}, {ClassStartDate}), versioning with effective dates, and local generation/export to PDF-like print output; e-signature is captured as typed name or drawn signature on Canvas with an attached tamper-evident hash (SHA-256 of document content + signer + timestamp), and signing workflows support initiate/sign/withdraw/void with complete local records. Data persists in LocalStorage for settings and session state and IndexedDB for larger objects (images, documents, analytics snapshots); export/import uses browser Blob downloads and file pickers to back up or restore the entire dataset, with optional passphrase-based AES encryption and masked display of sensitive fields (e.g., showing only last 4 characters of IDs) when viewed in shared environments.
